revision.comment: 園比屋武御嶽石門 を 園比屋武御嶽 へ移動

revision.text:
[[画像:Sonohyan.jpg|150px|right|thumb|園比屋武御嶽石門]] '''園比屋武御嶽'''（そのひゃんうたき）は[[16世紀]]の[[琉球王国]]・[[尚真]]王時代の[[御嶽 (沖縄)|御嶽]]で、[[沖縄県]][[那覇市]][[首里]]真和志町一丁目に位置する。
御嶽は国王が各地を巡航する旅に出る際必ず拝礼した場所であり、また[[聞得大君]]が就任するときにまず最初に拝礼した、いわば国家の聖地だった。
もともとはいまより広範であったが、現在では小学校の敷地となっている部分もあり、残されているのはその一部である。[[首里城]][[歓会門]]と[[守礼門]]との間にある'''園比屋武御嶽石門'''（そのひゃんうたきいしもん）がこの御嶽の礼拝所である。
園比屋武御嶽石門は、[[1519年]]に[[第二尚氏王統]]第3代王の[[尚真]]のときに造られた石門。[[オヤケ・アカハチ]]の乱で、王府軍が[[八重山]]へ出兵したさいに、将の一人であった大里親方に見込まれ首里に連れてこられた[[西塘]]により創建されたとされる。
[[沖縄戦]]において王城とともに戦禍を被り、無惨な姿となるがのち[[1957年]]に復元。さらにそのご旧石門の残欠を再利用しての修復作業が行われた。現在の石門を注意深く見てみると、明らかに摩耗の度合いが異なる部分があるが、このためである。園比屋武御嶽石門は[[2000年]]11月[[首里城]]跡などとともに、[[琉球王国のグスク及び関連遺産群]]として[[ユネスコ]]の[[世界遺産]]（文化遺産）にも登録されている。
[[Category:沖縄県の建築物・観光名所|そのひやんうたきいしもん]] [[category:日本の世界遺産|そのひやんうたきいしもん]] [[Category:沖縄の歴史|そのひやんうたきいしもん]]

もともとはいまより広範な森であったが、現在では小学校の敷地となっている部分もあり、残されているのはその一部である。[[首里城]][[歓会門]]と[[守礼門]]との間にある'''園比屋武御嶽石門'''（そのひゃんうたきいしもん）がこの御嶽の礼拝所である。

[[沖縄戦]]において王城とともに戦禍を被り、無惨な姿となるがのち[[1957年]]に復元。さらにそのご旧石門の残欠を再利用しての修復作業が行われた。現在の石門を注意深く見てみると、明らかに摩耗の度合いが異なる部分があるが、このためである。園比屋武御嶽石門は[[2年]]11月[[首里城]]跡などとともに、[[琉球王国のグスク及び関連遺産群]]として[[ユネスコ]]の[[世界遺産]]（文化遺産）にも登録されている。
{{琉球王国のグスク及び関連遺産群}}

[[沖縄戦]]において王城とともに戦禍を被り、無惨な姿となるがのち[[1957年]]に復元。さらにそのご旧石門の残欠を再利用しての修復作業が行われた。現在の石門を注意深く見てみると、明らかに摩耗の度合いが異なる部分があるが、このためである。園比屋武御嶽石門は[[2000年]][[11月]][[首里城]]跡などとともに、[[琉球王国のグスク及び関連遺産群]]として[[ユネスコ]]の[[世界遺産]]（文化遺産）にも登録されている。

[[Image:Sonohyan Utaki ishi-mon.jpg|250px|right|thumb|園比屋武御嶽石門]] '''園比屋武御嶽'''（そのひゃんうたき）は[[16世紀]]の[[琉球王国]]・[[尚真]]王時代の[[御嶽 (沖縄)|御嶽]]で、[[沖縄県]][[那覇市]][[首里]]真和志町一丁目に位置する。沖縄県指定史跡。
== 概要 == 石門の背後にある森が'''園比屋武御嶽'''である。この御嶽は国王が各地を巡航する旅に出る際必ず拝礼した場所であり、また[[聞得大君]]が就任するときにまず最初に拝礼した、いわば国家の[[聖地]]だった。王家尚氏ゆかりの島である伊平屋島の神「田の上のソノヒヤブ」を勧請し、祭っている。
もともとはいまより広範な森であったが、現在では小学校の敷地となっている部分もあり、残されているのはその一部である。[[首里城]][[歓会門]]と[[守礼門]]との間にある'''園比屋武御嶽石門'''（そのひゃんうたきいしもん）がこの御嶽の礼拝所である。
園比屋武御嶽石門は、[[1519年]]に[[第二尚氏王統]]第3代王の[[尚真]]のときに造られた石門。[[オヤケアカハチの乱]]で、王府軍が[[八重山]]へ出兵したさいに、将の一人であった[[大里親方]]に見込まれ首里に連れてこられた[[西塘]]により創建されたとされる。
[[沖縄戦]]において王城とともに戦禍を被り、無惨な姿となるがのち[[1957年]]に復元。さらにそのご旧石門の残欠を再利用しての修復作業が行われた。現在の石門を注意深く見てみると、明らかに摩耗の度合いが異なる部分があるが、このためである。園比屋武御嶽石門は[[2000年]][[11月]][[首里城]]跡などとともに、[[琉球王国のグスク及び関連遺産群]]として[[国際連合教育科学文化機関|ユネスコ]]の[[世界遺産]]（文化遺産）にも登録されている。
== 関連項目 == {{Commonscat|Sonohyan-utaki}} * [[御嶽 (沖縄)|御嶽]] * [[琉球王国のグスク及び関連遺産群]]
{{琉球王国のグスク及び関連遺産群}}

[[Image:Sonohyan Utaki ishi-mon.jpg|250px|right|thumb|園比屋武御嶽石門]] '''園比屋武御嶽'''（そのひゃんうたき）は[[16世紀]]の[[琉球王国]]・[[尚真]]王時代の[[御嶽 (沖縄)|御嶽]]で、[[沖縄県]][[那覇市]][[首里]]真和志町一丁目に位置する。沖縄県指定史跡。
== 概要 == 石門の背後にある森が'''園比屋武御嶽'''である。この御嶽は国王が各地を巡航する旅に出る際必ず拝礼した場所であり、また[[聞得大君]]が就任するときにまず最初に拝礼した、いわば国家の[[聖地]]だった。王家尚氏ゆかりの島である伊平屋島の神「田の上のソノヒヤブ」を勧請し、祭っている。 もともとは今より広範な森であったが、現在では小学校の敷地となっている部分もあり、残されているのはその一部である。
[[首里城]][[歓会門]]と[[守礼門]]との間にある'''園比屋武御嶽石門'''（そのひゃんうたきいしもん）がこの御嶽の礼拝所である。 園比屋武御嶽石門は、[[1519年]]に[[第二尚氏王統]]第3代王の[[尚真]]のときに造られた。[[オヤケアカハチの乱]]（1500年）で、王府軍が[[八重山]]へ出兵したさいに、将の一人であった[[大里親方]]に見込まれ首里に連れてこられた[[西塘]]という人物により創建されたとされる。
[[沖縄戦]]において王城とともに戦禍を被り、無惨な姿となるがのち[[1957年]]に復元。さらにそのご旧石門の残欠を再利用しての修復作業が行われた。現在の石門を注意深く見てみると、明らかに摩耗の度合いが異なる部分があるが、このためである。園比屋武御嶽石門は1999年に国の重要文化財に指定され、[[2000年]][[11月]][[首里城]]跡などとともに、[[琉球王国のグスク及び関連遺産群]]として[[国際連合教育科学文化機関|ユネスコ]]の[[世界遺産]]（文化遺産）にも登録されている。
== 関連項目 == {{Commonscat|Sonohyan-utaki}} * [[御嶽 (沖縄)|御嶽]] * [[琉球王国のグスク及び関連遺産群]]
{{琉球王国のグスク及び関連遺産群}}

[[Image:Sonohyan Utaki ishi-mon.jpg|250px|right|thumb|園比屋武御嶽石門]] '''園比屋武御嶽'''（そのひゃんうたき）は[[16世紀]]の[[琉球王国]]・[[尚真]]王時代の[[御嶽 (沖縄)|御嶽]]で、[[沖縄県]][[那覇市]][[首里]]真和志町一丁目に位置する。沖縄県指定史跡。
== 概要 == 石門の背後にある森が'''園比屋武御嶽'''である。この御嶽は国王が各地を巡航する旅に出る際必ず拝礼した場所であり、また[[聞得大君]]が就任するときにまず最初に拝礼した、いわば国家の[[聖地]]だった。王家尚氏ゆかりの島である伊平屋島の神「田の上のソノヒヤブ」を勧請し、祭っている。 もともとは今より広範な森であったが、現在では小学校の敷地となっている部分もあり、残されているのはその一部である。
[[首里城]][[歓会門]]と[[守礼門]]との間にある'''園比屋武御嶽石門'''（そのひゃんうたきいしもん）がこの御嶽の礼拝所である。 園比屋武御嶽石門は、[[1519年]]に[[第二尚氏王統]]第3代王の[[尚真]]のときに造られた。[[オヤケアカハチの乱]]（1500年）で、王府軍が[[八重山]]へ出兵したさいに、将の一人であった[[大里親方]]に見込まれ首里に連れてこられた[[西塘]]という人物により創建されたとされる。
[[沖縄戦]]において王城とともに戦禍を被り、無惨な姿となるがのち[[1957年]]に復元。さらにそのご旧石門の残欠を再利用しての修復作業が行われた。現在の石門を注意深く見てみると、明らかに摩耗の度合いが異なる部分があるが、このためである。園比屋武御嶽石門は1999年に国の重要文化財に指定され、[[2000年]][[11月]][[首里城]]跡などとともに、[[琉球王国のグスク及び関連遺産群]]として[[国際連合教育科学文化機関|ユネスコ]]の[[世界遺産]]（文化遺産）にも登録されている。
また、[[西塘]]によって作られた竹富島の国仲御嶽は、園比屋武御嶽の神を勧請した御嶽である。

[[Image:Sonohyan Utaki ishi-mon.jpg|250px|right|thumb|園比屋武御嶽石門]] '''園比屋武御嶽'''（そのひゃんうたき）は[[16世紀]]の[[琉球王国]]・[[尚真]]王時代の[[御嶽 (沖縄)|御嶽]]で、[[沖縄県]][[那覇市]][[首里]]真和志町一丁目に位置する。沖縄県指定史跡。
== 概要 == 石門の背後にある森が'''園比屋武御嶽'''である。この御嶽は国王が各地を巡航する旅に出る際必ず拝礼した場所であり、また[[聞得大君]]が就任するときにまず最初に拝礼した、いわば国家の[[聖地]]だった。王家尚氏ゆかりの島である伊平屋島の神「田の上のソノヒヤブ」を勧請し、祭っている。 もともとは今より広範な森であったが、現在では小学校の敷地となっている部分もあり、残されているのはその一部である。

昭和8年1月23日旧国宝に指定、[[沖縄戦]]において王城などとともに戦禍を被り指定解除、無惨な姿となるがのち[[1957年]]に復元。さらにその後旧石門の残欠を再利用しての修復作業が行われた。現在の石門を注意深く見てみると、明らかに摩耗の度合いが異なる部分があるが、このためである。園比屋武御嶽石門は1999年に国の重要文化財に再指定され、[[2000年]][[11月]][[首里城]]跡などとともに、[[琉球王国のグスク及び関連遺産群]]として[[国際連合教育科学文化機関|ユネスコ]]の[[世界遺産]]（文化遺産）にも登録された。

== 概要 == 石門の背後にある森が'''園比屋武御嶽'''である。この御嶽は国王が各地を巡航する旅に出る際必ず拝礼した場所であり、また[[聞得大君]]が就任するときにまず最初に拝礼した、いわば国家の[[聖地]]だった。王家尚氏ゆかりの島である伊平屋島の神「田の上のソノヒヤブ」を勧請し、祭っている。 もともとは今より広範な森であったが、現在では小学校の敷地となっている部分もあり、残されているのはその一部である。
[[首里城]][[歓会門]]と[[守礼門]]との間にある'''園比屋武御嶽石門'''（そのひゃんうたきいしもん）がこの御嶽の礼拝所である。 園比屋武御嶽石門は、[[1519年]]に[[第二尚氏王統]]第3代王の[[尚真]]のときに造られた。[[オヤケアカハチの乱]]（1500年）で、王府軍が[[八重山列島|八重山]]へ出兵したさいに、将の一人であった[[大里親方]]に見込まれ首里に連れてこられた[[西塘 (琉球の人物)|西塘]]により創建されたという。
昭和8年1月23日旧国宝に指定、[[沖縄戦]]において王城などとともに戦禍を被り指定解除、無惨な姿となるがのち[[1957年]]に復元。さらにその後旧石門の残欠を再利用しての修復作業が行われた。現在の石門を注意深く見てみると、明らかに摩耗の度合いが異なる部分があるが、このためである。園比屋武御嶽石門は1999年に国の重要文化財に再指定され、[[2000年]][[11月]][[首里城]]跡などとともに、[[琉球王国のグスク及び関連遺産群]]として[[国際連合教育科学文化機関|ユネスコ]]の[[世界遺産]]（文化遺産）にも登録された。
また、西塘によって作られた竹富島の国仲御嶽は、園比屋武御嶽の神を勧請した御嶽である。
== 関連項目 == {{Commonscat|Sonohyan-utaki}} * [[御嶽 (沖縄)|御嶽]] * [[琉球王国のグスク及び関連遺産群]]

[[ファイル:Sonohyan Utaki ishi-mon.jpg|250px|right|thumb|園比屋武御嶽石門]] [[ファイル:Sonohyan-utaki.jpeg|right|thumb|250px|石門後方にある森（御嶽）]] '''園比屋武御嶽'''（そのひゃんうたき）は[[16世紀]]の[[琉球王国]]・[[尚真]]王時代の[[御嶽 (沖縄)|御嶽]]で、[[沖縄県]][[那覇市]][[首里]]真和志町一丁目に位置する。沖縄県指定史跡。
== 概要 == 石門の背後にある森が'''園比屋武御嶽'''である。この御嶽は国王が各地を巡航する旅に出る際必ず拝礼した場所であり、また[[聞得大君]]が就任する時にまず最初に拝礼した、いわば国家の[[聖地]]だった。王家尚氏ゆかりの島である伊平屋島の神「田の上のソノヒヤブ」を勧請し、祭っている。もともとは今よりも広範な森であったが、現在では小学校の敷地となっている部分もあり、残されているのは一部である。
[[首里城]][[歓会門]]と[[守礼門]]との間にある'''園比屋武御嶽石門'''（そのひゃんうたきいしもん）がこの御嶽の礼拝所である。園比屋武御嶽石門は、[[1519年]]に[[第二尚氏王統]]第3代王の[[尚真]]のときに造られた。[[オヤケアカハチの乱]]（1500年）で王府軍が[[八重山列島|八重山]]へ出兵した際に、将の一人であった[[大里親方]]に見込まれ首里に連れてこられた[[西塘 (琉球の人物)|西塘]]により創建されたという。
1933年1月23日旧国宝に指定されたが、[[沖縄戦]]の戦禍によって王城などとともに荒廃し、指定解除。[[1957年]]に復元され、さらにその後旧石門の残欠を再利用して修復作業が行われた。現在の石門を注意深く見てみると、明らかに摩耗の度合いが異なる部分があるのは、このためである。園比屋武御嶽石門は1972年にあらためて国の重要文化財に指定され、[[2000年]][[11月]][[首里城]]跡などとともに、[[琉球王国のグスク及び関連遺産群]]として[[国際連合教育科学文化機関|ユネスコ]]の[[世界遺産]]（文化遺産）にも登録された。
また、西塘によって作られた竹富島の国仲御嶽は、園比屋武御嶽の神を勧請した御嶽である。
== 関連項目 == {{Commonscat|Sonohyan-utaki}} * [[御嶽 (沖縄)|御嶽]] * [[琉球王国のグスク及び関連遺産群]]
{{琉球王国のグスク及び関連遺産群}} {{Coord|26|13|05.1|N|127|43|02.5|E|display=title|region:JP_type:landmark}} {{DEFAULTSORT:そのひやんうたき}} [[Category:沖縄県の建築物]] [[Category:沖縄県の観光地]] [[category:日本の世界遺産]] [[Category:琉球王国]] [[Category:那覇市の歴史]] [[Category:沖縄県の重要文化財]] [[Category:都道府県指定史跡]]

{{出典の明記|date=2014年1月14日 (火) 00:32 (UTC)|ソートキー=沖縄そのひやんうたき}} [[ファイル:Naha Shuri Castle04bs3200.jpg|250px|right|thumb|園比屋武御嶽石門]] [[ファイル:Sonohyan-utaki.jpeg|right|thumb|250px|石門後方にある森（御嶽）]] '''園比屋武御嶽'''（そのひゃんうたき）は[[16世紀]]の[[琉球王国]]・[[尚真]]王時代の[[御嶽 (沖縄)|御嶽]]で、[[沖縄県]][[那覇市]][[首里]]真和志町一丁目に位置する。沖縄県指定史跡。
== 概要 == 石門の背後にある森が'''園比屋武御嶽'''である。この御嶽は国王が各地を巡航する旅に出る際必ず拝礼した場所であり、また[[聞得大君]]が就任する時にまず最初に拝礼した、いわば国家の[[聖地]]だった。王家尚氏ゆかりの島である伊平屋島の神「田の上のソノヒヤブ」を勧請し、祭っている。もともとは今よりも広範な森であったが、現在では小学校の敷地となっている部分もあり、残されているのは一部である。
[[首里城]][[歓会門]]と[[守礼門]]との間にある'''園比屋武御嶽石門'''（そのひゃんうたきいしもん）がこの御嶽の礼拝所である。園比屋武御嶽石門は、[[1519年]]に[[第二尚氏王統]]第3代王の[[尚真]]のときに造られた。[[オヤケアカハチの乱]]（1500年）で王府軍が[[八重山列島|八重山]]へ出兵した際に、将の一人であった[[大里親方]]に見込まれ首里に連れてこられた[[西塘 (琉球の人物)|西塘]]により創建されたという。
1933年1月23日旧国宝に指定されたが、[[沖縄戦]]の戦禍によって王城などとともに荒廃し、指定解除。[[1957年]]に復元され、さらにその後旧石門の残欠を再利用して修復作業が行われた。現在の石門を注意深く見てみると、明らかに摩耗の度合いが異なる部分があるのは、このためである。園比屋武御嶽石門は1972年にあらためて国の重要文化財に指定され、[[2000年]][[11月]][[首里城]]跡などとともに、[[琉球王国のグスク及び関連遺産群]]として[[国際連合教育科学文化機関|ユネスコ]]の[[世界遺産]]（文化遺産）にも登録された。
また、西塘によって作られた竹富島の国仲御嶽は、園比屋武御嶽の神を勧請した御嶽である。

revision.text:
{{出典の明記|date=2014年1月14日 (火) 00:32 (UTC)|ソートキー=沖縄そのひやんうたき}} [[ファイル:Naha Shuri Castle04bs3200.jpg|250px|right|thumb|園比屋武御嶽石門]] [[ファイル:Sonohyan-utaki.jpeg|right|thumb|250px|石門後方にある森（御嶽）]] '''園比屋武御嶽'''（そのひゃんうたき）は[[16世紀]]の[[琉球王国]]・[[尚真]]王時代の[[御嶽 (沖縄)|御嶽]]で、[[沖縄県]][[那覇市]][[首里]]真和志町一丁目に位置する。沖縄県指定史跡。
== 概要 == 石門の背後にある森が'''園比屋武御嶽'''である。この御嶽は国王が各地を巡航する旅に出る際必ず拝礼した場所であり、また[[聞得大君]]が就任する時にまず最初に拝礼した、いわば国家の[[聖地]]だった。王家尚氏ゆかりの島である伊平屋島の神「田の上のソノヒヤブ」を勧請し、祭っている。もともとは今よりも広範な森であったが、現在では小学校の敷地となっている部分もあり、残されているのは一部である。
[[首里城]][[歓会門]]と[[守礼門]]との間にある'''園比屋武御嶽石門'''（そのひゃんうたきいしもん）がこの御嶽の礼拝所である。園比屋武御嶽石門は、[[1519年]]に[[第二尚氏王統]]第3代王の[[尚真]]のときに造られた。[[オヤケアカハチの乱]]（1500年）で王府軍が[[八重山列島|八重山]]へ出兵した際に、将の一人であった[[大里親方]]に見込まれ首里に連れてこられた[[西塘 (琉球の人物)|西塘]]により創建されたという。
[[1933年]][[1月23日]]旧国宝に指定されたが、[[沖縄戦]]の戦禍によって王城などとともに荒廃し、指定解除。[[1957年]]に復元され、さらにその後旧石門の残欠を再利用して修復作業が行われた。現在の石門を注意深く見てみると、明らかに摩耗の度合いが異なる部分があるのは、このためである。園比屋武御嶽石門は1972年にあらためて国の重要文化財に指定され、[[2000年]][[11月]][[首里城]]跡などとともに、[[琉球王国のグスク及び関連遺産群]]として[[国際連合教育科学文化機関|ユネスコ]]の[[世界遺産]]（文化遺産）にも登録された。
また、西塘によって作られた竹富島の国仲御嶽は、園比屋武御嶽の神を勧請した御嶽である。
== 関連項目 == {{Commonscat|Sonohyan-utaki}} * [[御嶽 (沖縄)|御嶽]] * [[琉球王国のグスク及び関連遺産群]]

[[首里城]][[歓会門]]と[[守礼門]]との間にある'''園比屋武御嶽石門'''（そのひゃんうたきいしもん）がこの御嶽の礼拝所である。園比屋武御嶽石門は、[[1519年]]に[[第二尚氏王統]]第3代王の[[尚真]]のときに造られた。[[オヤケアカハチの乱]]（1500年）で王府軍が[[八重山列島|八重山]]へ出兵した際に、将の一人であった[[大里親方]]に見込まれ首里に連れてこられた[[西塘 (琉球の人物)|西塘]]により創建されたという。

また、西塘によって作られた竹富島の国仲御嶽は、園比屋武御嶽の神を勧請した御嶽である。

[[1933年]]（[[昭和]]8年）[[1月23日]]に旧国宝に指定されたが、[[沖縄戦]]の戦禍によって王城などとともに荒廃し、指定解除。[[1957年]]（昭和32年）に復元され、さらにその後旧石門の残欠を再利用して修復作業が行われた。現在の石門を注意深く見てみると、明らかに摩耗の度合いが異なる部分があるのは、このためである。園比屋武御嶽石門は[[1972年]]（昭和47年）にあらためて国の重要文化財に指定され、[[2000年]]（[[平成]]12年）[[11月]][[首里城]]跡などとともに、[[琉球王国のグスク及び関連遺産群]]として[[国際連合教育科学文化機関|ユネスコ]]の[[世界遺産]]（文化遺産）にも登録された<ref>{{Cite web |title=World Heritage Committee Inscribes 61 New Sites on World Heritage List |url=https://whc.unesco.org/en/news/184/ |website=UNESCO World Heritage Centre |access-date=2022-05-10 |language=en |first=UNESCO World Heritage |last=Centre}}</ref>。

[[ファイル:Naha Shuri Castle04bs3200.jpg|250px|right|thumb|園比屋武御嶽石門]] [[ファイル:Sonohyan-utaki.jpeg|right|thumb|250px|石門後方にある森（御嶽）]] '''園比屋武御嶽'''（そのひゃんうたき）は[[16世紀]]の[[琉球王国]]・[[尚真王]]時代の[[御嶽 (沖縄)|御嶽]]で、[[沖縄県]][[那覇市]][[首里]]真和志町一丁目に位置する。沖縄県指定史跡。
== 概要 == 石門の背後にある森が'''園比屋武御嶽'''である。この御嶽は国王が各地を巡航する旅に出る際必ず拝礼した場所であり、また[[聞得大君]]が就任する時にまず最初に拝礼した、いわば国家の[[聖地]]だった。王家尚氏ゆかりの島である伊平屋島の神「田の上のソノヒヤブ」を勧請し、祭っている。もともとは今よりも広範な森であったが、現在では小学校の敷地となっている部分もあり、残されているのは一部である。
== 園比屋武御嶽石門 == [[首里城]]の[[歓会門]]と[[守礼門]]との間にある'''園比屋武御嶽石門'''（そのひゃんうたきいしもん）がこの御嶽の礼拝所である。園比屋武御嶽石門は、[[1519年]]に[[第二尚氏王統]]第3代王の[[尚真]]のときに造られた。[[オヤケアカハチの乱]]（1500年）で王府軍が[[八重山列島|八重山]]へ出兵した際に、将の一人であった[[大里親方]]に見込まれ首里に連れてこられた[[西塘 (琉球の人物)|西塘]]により創建されたという。
[[1933年]]（[[昭和]]8年）[[1月23日]]に旧国宝に指定されたが、[[沖縄戦]]の戦禍によって王城などとともに荒廃し、指定解除。[[1957年]]（昭和32年）に復元され、さらにその後旧石門の残欠を再利用して修復作業が行われた。現在の石門を注意深く見てみると、明らかに摩耗の度合いが異なる部分があるのは、このためである。園比屋武御嶽石門は[[1972年]]（昭和47年）にあらためて国の重要文化財に指定され、[[2000年]]（[[平成]]12年）[[11月]][[首里城]]跡などとともに、[[琉球王国のグスク及び関連遺産群]]として[[国際連合教育科学文化機関|ユネスコ]]の[[世界遺産]]（文化遺産）にも登録された<ref>{{Cite web |title=World Heritage Committee Inscribes 61 New Sites on World Heritage List |url=https://whc.unesco.org/en/news/184/ |website=UNESCO World Heritage Centre |access-date=2022-05-10 |language=en |first=UNESCO World Heritage |last=Centre}}</ref>。
また、西塘によって創建された竹富島の国仲御嶽は、園比屋武御嶽の神を勧請した御嶽である。
== 出典 == <references />

[[1933年]]（[[昭和]]8年）[[1月23日]]に旧国宝に指定されたが、[[沖縄戦]]の戦禍によって王城などとともに荒廃し、指定解除。[[1957年]]（昭和32年）に復元され、さらにその後旧石門の残欠を再利用して修復作業が行われた。現在の石門を注意深く見てみると、明らかに摩耗の度合いが異なる部分があるのは、このためである。園比屋武御嶽石門は[[1972年]]（昭和47年）にあらためて国の重要文化財に指定され、[[2000年]]（[[平成]]12年）[[11月]][[首里城]]跡などとともに、[[琉球王国のグスク及び関連遺産群]]として[[国際連合教育科学文化機関|ユネスコ]]の[[世界遺産]]（文化遺産）にも登録された<ref>{{Cite web |title=World Heritage Committee Inscribes 61 New Sites on World Heritage List |url=https://whc.unesco.org/en/news/184/ |website=UNESCO World Heritage Centre |access-date=2022-05-10 |language=en |first=UNESCO World Heritage |last=Centre}}</ref>。
また、西塘によって創建された竹富島の国仲御嶽は、園比屋武御嶽の神を勧請した御嶽である。

revision.text:
[[File:Naha Shuri Castle04bs3200.jpg|250px|right|thumb|Sonohyan-utaki Ishimon]] [[File:Sonohyan-utaki.jpeg|right|thumb|250px|The forest (utaki) behind the stone gate]]
'''Sonohyan-utaki''' (園比屋武御嶽) is an {{ill|utaki|ja|御嶽}}, or sacred site, from the 16th century during the {{ill|Ryukyu Kingdom|ja|琉球王国}}'s {{ill|King Shō Shin|ja|尚真王}} era. It is located in {{ill|Shuri, Naha|ja|首里}}, {{ill|Naha, Okinawa|ja|那覇市}}, Okinawa Prefecture, and is designated as an Okinawa Prefecture Historic Site.
== Overview == The forest behind the stone gate is called '''Sonohyan-utaki'''. This utaki was a sacred place where the king would always pray before departing on journeys. Additionally, the {{ill|Kikoe-ōgimi|ja|聞得大君}} performed her first prayer here upon assuming her position, making it a national {{ill|sacred site|ja|聖地}}. The shrine enshrines the deity "Tanoue no Sonohiyabu" from Iheya Island, which was closely linked to the ruling {{ill|Shō family|ja|尚氏}}. Originally, it was a much larger forest, but parts of it have since been used for a school, leaving only a portion intact.

== Overview == The forest behind the stone gate is called '''Sonohyan-utaki'''. This utaki was a sacred place where the king would always pray before departing on journeys. Additionally, the {{ill|Kikoe-ōgimi|ja|聞得大君}} performed her first prayer here upon assuming her position, making it a national {{ill|sacred site|ja|聖地}}. The shrine enshrines the deity "Tanoue no Sonohiyabu" from Iheya Island, which was closely linked to the ruling {{ill|Shō family|ja|尚氏}}. Originally, it was a much larger forest, but parts of it have since been used for a school, leaving only a portion intact.
== Sonohyan-utaki Ishimon == Located between {{ill|Kankaimon|ja|歓会門}} and {{ill|Shureimon|ja|守礼門}} at {{ill|Shuri Castle|ja|首里城}}, the '''Sonohyan-utaki Ishimon''' (園比屋武御嶽石門) serves as the worship site of the utaki. This stone gate was built in [[1519]] during the reign of {{ill|King Shō Shin|ja|尚真}}, the third monarch of the {{ill|Second Shō Dynasty|ja|第二尚氏王統}}. It is said to have been established by {{ill|Sai Tō|ja|西塘}}, who was brought to Shuri by {{ill|Ōzato Aji|ja|大里親方}} after the {{ill|Oyake Akahachi Rebellion|ja|オヤケアカハチの乱}} in 1500, during which the royal army was sent to the {{ill|Yaeyama Islands|ja|八重山列島}}.
Originally designated as a [[National Treasure of Japan|National Treasure]] on [[January 23, 1933]], the stone gate, along with the royal castle, was severely damaged during the [[Battle of Okinawa]], leading to the loss of its designation. However, in [[1957]], it was reconstructed, and later, parts of the original stonework were incorporated into restoration efforts. Close inspection of the gate reveals areas with differing degrees of wear due to these reconstruction efforts. In [[1972]], the gate was re-designated as an [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]. In [[2000]], along with the {{ill|Shuri Castle ruins|ja|首里城}}, it was included in the {{ill|Gusuku Sites and Related Properties of the Kingdom of Ryukyu|ja|琉球王国のグスク及び関連遺産群}}, a [[UNESCO]] [[World Heritage Site (UNESCO)|World Heritage Site]]<ref>{{Cite web |title=World Heritage Committee Inscribes 61 New Sites on World Heritage List |url=https://whc.unesco.org/en/news/184/ |website=UNESCO World Heritage Centre |access-date=2022-05-10 |language=en |first=UNESCO World Heritage |last=Centre}}</ref>.

{{infobox religious building |name=Sonohyan-utaki
|image=File:Naha Shuri Castle04bs3200.jpg |caption=Sonohyan-utaki Ishimon }} [[File:Sonohyan-utaki.jpeg|right|thumb|250px|The forest (utaki) behind the stone gate]]
'''Sonohyan-utaki''' (園比屋武御嶽) is an {{ill|utaki|ja|御嶽}}, or sacred site, from the 16th century during the {{ill|Ryukyu Kingdom|ja|琉球王国}}'s {{ill|King Shō Shin|ja|尚真王}} era. It is located in {{ill|Shuri, Naha|ja|首里}}, {{ill|Naha, Okinawa|ja|那覇市}}, Okinawa Prefecture, and is designated as an Okinawa Prefecture Historic Site.
== Overview == The forest behind the stone gate is called '''Sonohyan-utaki'''. This utaki was a sacred place where the king would always pray before departing on journeys. Additionally, the {{ill|Kikoe-ōgimi|ja|聞得大君}} performed her first prayer here upon assuming her position, making it a national {{ill|sacred site|ja|聖地}}. The shrine enshrines the deity "Tanoue no Sonohiyabu" from Iheya Island, which was closely linked to the ruling {{ill|Shō family|ja|尚氏}}. Originally, it was a much larger forest, but parts of it have since been used for a school, leaving only a portion intact.
== Sonohyan-utaki Ishimon == Located between {{ill|Kankaimon|ja|歓会門}} and {{ill|Shureimon|ja|守礼門}} at {{ill|Shuri Castle|ja|首里城}}, the '''Sonohyan-utaki Ishimon''' (園比屋武御嶽石門) serves as the {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site}} of the utaki. This stone gate was built in [[1519]] during the reign of {{ill|King Shō Shin|ja|尚真}}, the third monarch of the {{ill|Second Shō Dynasty|ja|第二尚氏王統}}. It is said to have been established by {{ill|Sai Tō|ja|西塘}}, who was brought to Shuri by {{ill|Ōzato Aji|ja|大里親方}} after the {{ill|Oyake Akahachi Rebellion|ja|オヤケアカハチの乱}} in 1500, during which the royal army was sent to the {{ill|Yaeyama Islands|ja|八重山列島}}.
Originally designated as a [[National Treasure of Japan|National Treasure]] on [[January 23, 1933]], the stone gate, along with the royal castle, was severely damaged during the [[Battle of Okinawa]], leading to the loss of its designation. However, in [[1957]], it was reconstructed, and later, parts of the original stonework were incorporated into restoration efforts. Close inspection of the gate reveals areas with differing degrees of wear due to these reconstruction efforts. In [[1972]], the gate was re-designated as an [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]. In [[2000]], along with the {{ill|Shuri Castle ruins|ja|首里城}}, it was included in the {{ill|Gusuku Sites and Related Properties of the Kingdom of Ryukyu|ja|琉球王国のグスク及び関連遺産群}}, a [[UNESCO]] [[World Heritage Site (UNESCO)|World Heritage Site]]<ref>{{Cite web |title=World Heritage Committee Inscribes 61 New Sites on World Heritage List |url=https://whc.unesco.org/en/news/184/ |website=UNESCO World Heritage Centre |access-date=2022-05-10 |language=en |first=UNESCO World Heritage |last=Centre}}</ref>.
Additionally, the {{ill|Kunigami-utaki|ja|国仲御嶽}} on Taketomi Island, also founded by Sai Tō, enshrines the deity of Sonohyan-utaki.

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Sonohyan-utaki]][[cs:Sonohjan Utaki Išimon]][[de:Sonohyan-Utaki]][[en:Sonohyan-utaki]][[fr:Sonohyan-utaki]][[ja:園比屋武御嶽]][[tr:Sonohyan-utaki]][[vi:Sonohyan-utaki]][[zh:園比屋武御嶽]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q1053962]] Wikidata name: Sonohyan-utaki [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:31, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox religious building |name=Sonohyan-utaki
|image=File:Naha Shuri Castle04bs3200.jpg |caption=Sonohyan-utaki Ishimon }} [[File:Sonohyan-utaki.jpeg|right|thumb|250px|The forest (utaki) behind the stone gate]]
'''Sonohyan-utaki''' (園比屋武御嶽) is an [[:en:Ontake|utaki]], or sacred site, from the 16th century during the [[:en:Ryukyu Kingdom|Ryukyu Kingdom]]'s [[:en:Shō Shin|King Shō Shin]] era. It is located in [[:en:Shuri, Okinawa|Shuri, Naha]], [[:en:Naha|Naha, Okinawa]], Okinawa Prefecture, and is designated as an Okinawa Prefecture Historic Site.
== Overview == The forest behind the stone gate is called '''Sonohyan-utaki'''. This utaki was a sacred place where the king would always pray before departing on journeys. Additionally, the [[:en:Kikoe-ōgimi|Kikoe-ōgimi]] performed her first prayer here upon assuming her position, making it a national {{ill|sacred site|ja|聖地|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sacred site|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=sacred place|WD=Q4588528}}. The shrine enshrines the deity "Tanoue no Sonohiyabu" from Iheya Island, which was closely linked to the ruling {{ill|Shō family|ja|尚氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shō family|qq=|1=Shō family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q118441183}}. Originally, it was a much larger forest, but parts of it have since been used for a school, leaving only a portion intact.
== Sonohyan-utaki Ishimon == Located between {{ill|Kankaimon|ja|歓会門|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kankaimon|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and [[:en:Shureimon|Shureimon]] at [[:en:Shuri Castle|Shuri Castle]], the '''Sonohyan-utaki Ishimon''' (園比屋武御嶽石門) serves as the {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} of the utaki. This stone gate was built in [[:en:1519|1519]] during the reign of {{ill|King Shō Shin|ja|尚真|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/King Shō Shin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 尚真王|1=Shō Shin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q281765}}, the third monarch of the {{ill|Second Shō Dynasty|ja|第二尚氏王統|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Second Shō Dynasty|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 第二尚氏|1=Second Shō Dynasty|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11602290}}. It is said to have been established by {{ill|Sai Tō|ja|西塘|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sai Tō|qq=|1=Sai Tō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q29413439}}, who was brought to Shuri by {{ill|Ōzato Aji|ja|大里親方|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōzato Aji|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} after the {{ill|Oyake Akahachi Rebellion|ja|オヤケアカハチの乱|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyake Akahachi Rebellion|qq=|1=Oyake Akahachi rebellion|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11292491}} in 1500, during which the royal army was sent to the [[:en:Yaeyama Islands|Yaeyama Islands]].
Originally designated as a [[:en:National Treasure of Japan|National Treasure]] on [[January 23, 1933]], the stone gate, along with the royal castle, was severely damaged during the [[:en:Battle of Okinawa|Battle of Okinawa]], leading to the loss of its designation. However, in [[:en:1957|1957]], it was reconstructed, and later, parts of the original stonework were incorporated into restoration efforts. Close inspection of the gate reveals areas with differing degrees of wear due to these reconstruction efforts. In [[:en:1972|1972]], the gate was re-designated as an [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]. In [[:en:2000|2000]], along with the [[:en:Shuri Castle|Shuri Castle ruins]], it was included in the [[:en:Gusuku Sites and Related Properties of the Kingdom of Ryukyu|Gusuku Sites and Related Properties of the Kingdom of Ryukyu]], a [[:en:UNESCO|UNESCO]] [[World Heritage Site (UNESCO)|World Heritage Site]]<ref>{{Cite web |title=World Heritage Committee Inscribes 61 New Sites on World Heritage List |url=https://whc.unesco.org/en/news/184/ |website=UNESCO World Heritage Centre |access-date=2022-05-10 |language=en |first=UNESCO World Heritage |last=Centre}}</ref>.
Additionally, the {{ill|Kunigami-utaki|ja|国仲御嶽|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunigami-utaki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 六山|1=Mūyama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11392326}} on Taketomi Island, also founded by Sai Tō, enshrines the deity of Sonohyan-utaki.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == {{Commonscat|Sonohyan-utaki}} * [[:en:Ontake|Utaki]] * [[:en:Gusuku Sites and Related Properties of the Kingdom of Ryukyu|Gusuku Sites and Related Properties of the Kingdom of Ryukyu]] * {{OSM|n|656537494}}
{{Gusuku Sites and Related Properties of the Kingdom of Ryukyu}} {{Coord|26|13|05.1|N|127|43|02.5|E|display=title|region:JP-47_type:landmark|name=Sonohyan-utaki}}

|image=File:Naha Shuri Castle04bs3200.jpg |caption=Sonohyan-utaki Ishimon }} [[File:Sonohyan-utaki.jpeg|right|thumb|250px|The forest (utaki) behind the stone gate]]
'''Sonohyan-utaki''' (園比屋武御嶽) is an [[:en:Ontake|utaki]], or sacred site, from the 16th century during the [[:en:Ryukyu Kingdom|Ryukyu Kingdom]]'s [[:en:Shō Shin|King Shō Shin]] era. It is located in [[:en:Shuri, Okinawa|Shuri, Naha]], [[:en:Naha|Naha, Okinawa]], Okinawa Prefecture, and is designated as an Okinawa Prefecture Historic Site.
== Overview == The forest behind the stone gate is called '''Sonohyan-utaki'''. This utaki was a sacred place where the king would always pray before departing on journeys. Additionally, the [[:en:Kikoe-ōgimi|Kikoe-ōgimi]] performed her first prayer here upon assuming her position, making it a national {{ill|sacred site|ja|聖地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sacred site|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=sacred place|WD=Q4588528}}. The shrine enshrines the deity "Tanoue no Sonohiyabu" from Iheya Island, which was closely linked to the ruling {{ill|Shō family|ja|尚氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shō family|1=Shō family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q118441183}}. Originally, it was a much larger forest, but parts of it have since been used for a school, leaving only a portion intact.
== Sonohyan-utaki Ishimon == Located between {{ill|Kankaimon|ja|歓会門|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kankaimon|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and [[:en:Shureimon|Shureimon]] at [[:en:Shuri Castle|Shuri Castle]], the '''Sonohyan-utaki Ishimon''' (園比屋武御嶽石門) serves as the {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} of the utaki. This stone gate was built in [[:en:1519|1519]] during the reign of {{ill|King Shō Shin|ja|尚真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/King Shō Shin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 尚真王|1=Shō Shin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q281765}}, the third monarch of the {{ill|Second Shō Dynasty|ja|第二尚氏王統|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Second Shō Dynasty|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 第二尚氏|1=Second Shō Dynasty|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11602290}}. It is said to have been established by {{ill|Sai Tō|ja|西塘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sai Tō|1=Sai Tō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q29413439}}, who was brought to Shuri by {{ill|Ōzato Aji|ja|大里親方|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōzato Aji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} after the {{ill|Oyake Akahachi Rebellion|ja|オヤケアカハチの乱|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyake Akahachi Rebellion|1=Oyake Akahachi rebellion|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11292491}} in 1500, during which the royal army was sent to the [[:en:Yaeyama Islands|Yaeyama Islands]].
Originally designated as a [[:en:National Treasure of Japan|National Treasure]] on [[January 23, 1933]], the stone gate, along with the royal castle, was severely damaged during the [[:en:Battle of Okinawa|Battle of Okinawa]], leading to the loss of its designation. However, in [[:en:1957|1957]], it was reconstructed, and later, parts of the original stonework were incorporated into restoration efforts. Close inspection of the gate reveals areas with differing degrees of wear due to these reconstruction efforts. In [[:en:1972|1972]], the gate was re-designated as an [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]. In [[:en:2000|2000]], along with the [[:en:Shuri Castle|Shuri Castle ruins]], it was included in the [[:en:Gusuku Sites and Related Properties of the Kingdom of Ryukyu|Gusuku Sites and Related Properties of the Kingdom of Ryukyu]], a [[:en:UNESCO|UNESCO]] [[World Heritage Site (UNESCO)|World Heritage Site]]<ref>{{Cite web |title=World Heritage Committee Inscribes 61 New Sites on World Heritage List |url=https://whc.unesco.org/en/news/184/ |website=UNESCO World Heritage Centre |access-date=2022-05-10 |language=en |first=UNESCO World Heritage |last=Centre}}</ref>.
Additionally, the {{ill|Kunigami-utaki|ja|国仲御嶽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunigami-utaki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 六山|1=Mūyama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11392326}} on Taketomi Island, also founded by Sai Tō, enshrines the deity of Sonohyan-utaki.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Sonohyan-utaki]][[cs:Sonohjan Utaki Išimon]][[de:Sonohyan-Utaki]][[en:Sonohyan-utaki]][[fr:Sonohyan-utaki]][[ja:園比屋武御嶽]][[tr:Sonohyan-utaki]][[vi:Sonohyan-utaki]][[zh:園比屋武御嶽]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q1053962]] Wikidata name: Sonohyan-utaki [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:31, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox religious building |name=Sonohyan-utaki
|image=File:Naha Shuri Castle04bs3200.jpg |caption=Sonohyan-utaki Ishimon }} [[File:Sonohyan-utaki.jpeg|right|thumb|250px|The forest (utaki) behind the stone gate]]
'''Sonohyan-utaki''' (園比屋武御嶽) is an [[:en:Ontake|utaki]], or sacred site, from the 16th century during the [[:en:Ryukyu Kingdom|Ryukyu Kingdom]]'s [[:en:Shō Shin|King Shō Shin]] era. It is located in [[:en:Shuri, Okinawa|Shuri, Naha]], [[:en:Naha|Naha, Okinawa]], Okinawa Prefecture, and is designated as an Okinawa Prefecture Historic Site.
== Overview == The forest behind the stone gate is called '''Sonohyan-utaki'''. This utaki was a sacred place where the king would always pray before departing on journeys. Additionally, the [[:en:Kikoe-ōgimi|Kikoe-ōgimi]] performed her first prayer here upon assuming her position, making it a national {{ill|sacred site|ja|聖地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sacred site|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=sacred place|WD=Q4588528}}. The shrine enshrines the deity "Tanoue no Sonohiyabu" from Iheya Island, which was closely linked to the ruling {{ill|Shō family|ja|尚氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shō family|1=Shō family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q118441183}}. Originally, it was a much larger forest, but parts of it have since been used for a school, leaving only a portion intact.
== Sonohyan-utaki Ishimon == Located between {{ill|Kankaimon|ja|歓会門|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kankaimon|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and [[:en:Shureimon|Shureimon]] at [[:en:Shuri Castle|Shuri Castle]], the '''Sonohyan-utaki Ishimon''' (園比屋武御嶽石門) serves as the {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} of the utaki. This stone gate was built in [[:en:1519|1519]] during the reign of {{ill|King Shō Shin|ja|尚真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/King Shō Shin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 尚真王|1=Shō Shin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q281765}}, the third monarch of the {{ill|Second Shō Dynasty|ja|第二尚氏王統|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Second Shō Dynasty|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 第二尚氏|1=Second Shō Dynasty|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11602290}}. It is said to have been established by {{ill|Sai Tō|ja|西塘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sai Tō|1=Sai Tō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q29413439}}, who was brought to Shuri by {{ill|Ōzato Aji|ja|大里親方|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōzato Aji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} after the {{ill|Oyake Akahachi Rebellion|ja|オヤケアカハチの乱|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyake Akahachi Rebellion|1=Oyake Akahachi rebellion|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11292491}} in 1500, during which the royal army was sent to the [[:en:Yaeyama Islands|Yaeyama Islands]].
Originally designated as a [[:en:National Treasure of Japan|National Treasure]] on [[January 23, 1933]], the stone gate, along with the royal castle, was severely damaged during the [[:en:Battle of Okinawa|Battle of Okinawa]], leading to the loss of its designation. However, in [[:en:1957|1957]], it was reconstructed, and later, parts of the original stonework were incorporated into restoration efforts. Close inspection of the gate reveals areas with differing degrees of wear due to these reconstruction efforts. In [[:en:1972|1972]], the gate was re-designated as an [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]. In [[:en:2000|2000]], along with the [[:en:Shuri Castle|Shuri Castle ruins]], it was included in the [[:en:Gusuku Sites and Related Properties of the Kingdom of Ryukyu|Gusuku Sites and Related Properties of the Kingdom of Ryukyu]], a [[:en:UNESCO|UNESCO]] [[World Heritage Site (UNESCO)|World Heritage Site]]<ref>{{Cite web |title=World Heritage Committee Inscribes 61 New Sites on World Heritage List |url=https://whc.unesco.org/en/news/184/ |website=UNESCO World Heritage Centre |access-date=2022-05-10 |language=en |first=UNESCO World Heritage |last=Centre}}</ref>.
Additionally, the {{ill|Kunigami-utaki|ja|国仲御嶽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunigami-utaki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 六山|1=Mūyama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11392326}} on Taketomi Island, also founded by Sai Tō, enshrines the deity of Sonohyan-utaki.

It is located in [[:en:Shuri, Okinawa|Shuri, Naha]], [[:en:Naha|Naha, Okinawa]], Okinawa Prefecture, and is designated as an Okinawa Prefecture Historic Site.
== Overview == The forest behind the stone gate is called '''Sonohyan-utaki'''. This utaki was a sacred place where the king would always pray before departing on journeys. Additionally, the [[:en:Kikoe-ōgimi|Kikoe-ōgimi]] performed her first prayer here upon assuming her position, making it a national {{ill|sacred site|ja|聖地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sacred site|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=sacred place|WD=Q4588528}}. This stone gate was built in [[:en:1519|1519]] during the reign of {{ill|King Shō Shin|ja|尚真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/King Shō Shin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 尚真王|1=Shō Shin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q281765}}, the third monarch of the {{ill|Second Shō Dynasty|ja|第二尚氏王統|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Second Shō Dynasty|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 第二尚氏|1=Second Shō Dynasty|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11602290}}. It is said to have been established by {{ill|Sai Tō|ja|西塘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sai Tō|1=Sai Tō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q29413439}}, who was brought to Shuri by {{ill|Ōzato Aji|ja|大里親方|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōzato Aji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} after the {{ill|Oyake Akahachi Rebellion|ja|オヤケアカハチの乱|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyake Akahachi Rebellion|1=Oyake Akahachi rebellion|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11292491}} in 1500, during which the royal army was sent to the [[:en:Yaeyama Islands|Yaeyama Islands]].
Originally designated as a [[:en:National Treasure of Japan|National Treasure]] on [[January 23, 1933]], the stone gate, along with the royal castle, was severely damaged during the [[:en:Battle of Okinawa|Battle of Okinawa]], leading to the loss of its designation. However, in [[:en:1957|1957]], it was reconstructed, and later, parts of the original stonework were incorporated into restoration efforts. Close inspection of the gate reveals areas with differing degrees of wear due to these reconstruction efforts. In [[:en:1972|1972]], the gate was re-designated as an [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]. In [[:en:2000|2000]], along with the [[:en:Shuri Castle|Shuri Castle ruins]], it was included in the [[:en:Gusuku Sites and Related Properties of the Kingdom of Ryukyu|Gusuku Sites and Related Properties of the Kingdom of Ryukyu]], a [[:en:UNESCO|UNESCO]] [[World Heritage Site (UNESCO)|World Heritage Site]]<ref>{{Cite web |title=World Heritage Committee Inscribes 61 New Sites on World Heritage List |url=https://whc.unesco.org/en/news/184/ |website=UNESCO World Heritage Centre |access-date=2022-05-10 |language=en |first=UNESCO World Heritage |last=Centre}}</ref>.
Additionally, the {{ill|Kunigami-utaki|ja|国仲御嶽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunigami-utaki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 六山|1=Mūyama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11392326}} on Taketomi Island, also founded by Sai Tō, enshrines the deity of Sonohyan-utaki.

'''Sonohyan-utaki''' (園比屋武御嶽) is an [[:en:Ontake|utaki]], or sacred site, from the 16th century during the [[:en:Ryukyu Kingdom|Ryukyu Kingdom]]'s [[:en:Shō Shin|King Shō Shin]] era. It is located in [[:en:Shuri, Okinawa|Shuri, Naha]], [[:en:Naha|Naha, Okinawa]], Okinawa Prefecture, and is designated as an Okinawa Prefecture Historic Site.
== Overview == The forest behind the stone gate is called '''Sonohyan-utaki'''. This utaki was a sacred place where the king would always pray before departing on journeys. Additionally, the [[:en:Kikoe-ōgimi|Kikoe-ōgimi]] performed her first prayer here upon assuming her position, making it a national {{ill|sacred site|ja|聖地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sacred site|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=sacred place|WD=Q4588528}}. The shrine enshrines the deity "Tanoue no Sonohiyabu" from Iheya Island, which was closely linked to the ruling {{ill|Shō family|ja|尚氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shō family|1=Shō family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q118441183}}. Originally, it was a much larger forest, but parts of it have since been used for a school, leaving only a portion intact.

However, in [[:en:1957|1957]], it was reconstructed, and later, parts of the original stonework were incorporated into restoration efforts. Close inspection of the gate reveals areas with differing degrees of wear due to these reconstruction efforts. In [[:en:1972|1972]], the gate was re-designated as an [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]. In [[:en:2000|2000]], along with the [[:en:Shuri Castle|Shuri Castle ruins]], it was included in the [[:en:Gusuku Sites and Related Properties of the Kingdom of Ryukyu|Gusuku Sites and Related Properties of the Kingdom of Ryukyu]], a [[:en:UNESCO|UNESCO]] [[World Heritage Site (UNESCO)|World Heritage Site]]<ref>{{Cite web |title=World Heritage Committee Inscribes 61 New Sites on World Heritage List |url=https://whc.unesco.org/en/news/184/ |website=UNESCO World Heritage Centre |access-date=2022-05-10 |language=en |first=UNESCO World Heritage |last=Centre}}</ref>.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Sonohyan-utaki]][[cs:Sonohjan Utaki Išimon]][[de:Sonohyan-Utaki]][[en:Sonohyan-utaki]][[fr:Sonohyan-utaki]][[ja:園比屋武御嶽]][[tr:Sonohyan-utaki]][[vi:Sonohyan-utaki]][[zh:園比屋武御嶽]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Sonohyan-utaki]][[cs:Sonohjan Utaki Išimon]][[de:Sonohyan-Utaki]][[en:Sonohyan-utaki]][[fr:Sonohyan-utaki]][[ja:園比屋武御嶽]][[tr:Sonohyan-utaki]][[vi:Sonohyan-utaki]][[zh:園比屋武御嶽]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q1053962]] Wikidata name: Sonohyan-utaki [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:31, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox religious building |name=Sonohyan-utaki
|image=File:Naha Shuri Castle04bs3200.jpg |caption=Sonohyan-utaki Ishimon }} [[File:Sonohyan-utaki.jpeg|right|thumb|250px|The forest (utaki) behind the stone gate]]
'''Sonohyan-utaki''' (園比屋武御嶽) is an [[:en:Ontake|utaki]], or sacred site, from the 16th century during the [[:en:Ryukyu Kingdom|Ryukyu Kingdom]]'s [[:en:Shō Shin|King Shō Shin]] era. It is located in [[:en:Shuri, Okinawa|Shuri, Naha]], [[:en:Naha|Naha, Okinawa]], Okinawa Prefecture, and is designated as an Okinawa Prefecture Historic Site.
== Overview == The forest behind the stone gate is called '''Sonohyan-utaki'''. This utaki was a sacred place where the king would always pray before departing on journeys. Additionally, the [[:en:Kikoe-ōgimi|Kikoe-ōgimi]] performed her first prayer here upon assuming her position, making it a national {{ill|sacred site|ja|聖地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sacred site|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=sacred place|WD=Q4588528}}. The shrine enshrines the deity "Tanoue no Sonohiyabu" from Iheya Island, which was closely linked to the ruling {{ill|Shō family|ja|尚氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shō family|1=Shō family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q118441183}}. Originally, it was a much larger forest, but parts of it have since been used for a school, leaving only a portion intact.
== Sonohyan-utaki Ishimon == Located between {{ill|Kankaimon|ja|歓会門|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kankaimon|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and [[:en:Shureimon|Shureimon]] at [[:en:Shuri Castle|Shuri Castle]], the '''Sonohyan-utaki Ishimon''' (園比屋武御嶽石門) serves as the {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} of the utaki. This stone gate was built in [[:en:1519|1519]] during the reign of {{ill|King Shō Shin|ja|尚真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/King Shō Shin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 尚真王|1=Shō Shin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q281765}}, the third monarch of the {{ill|Second Shō Dynasty|ja|第二尚氏王統|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Second Shō Dynasty|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 第二尚氏|1=Second Shō Dynasty|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11602290}}. It is said to have been established by {{ill|Sai Tō|ja|西塘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sai Tō|1=Sai Tō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q29413439}}, who was brought to Shuri by {{ill|Ōzato Aji|ja|大里親方|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōzato Aji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} after the {{ill|Oyake Akahachi Rebellion|ja|オヤケアカハチの乱|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyake Akahachi Rebellion|1=Oyake Akahachi rebellion|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11292491}} in 1500, during which the royal army was sent to the [[:en:Yaeyama Islands|Yaeyama Islands]].
Originally designated as a [[:en:National Treasure of Japan|National Treasure]] on [[January 23, 1933]], the stone gate, along with the royal castle, was severely damaged during the [[:en:Battle of Okinawa|Battle of Okinawa]], leading to the loss of its designation. However, in [[:en:1957|1957]], it was reconstructed, and later, parts of the original stonework were incorporated into restoration efforts. Close inspection of the gate reveals areas with differing degrees of wear due to these reconstruction efforts. In [[:en:1972|1972]], the gate was re-designated as an [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]. In [[:en:2000|2000]], along with the [[:en:Shuri Castle|Shuri Castle ruins]], it was included in the [[:en:Gusuku Sites and Related Properties of the Kingdom of Ryukyu|Gusuku Sites and Related Properties of the Kingdom of Ryukyu]], a [[:en:UNESCO|UNESCO]] [[World Heritage Site (UNESCO)|World Heritage Site]]<ref>{{Cite web |title=World Heritage Committee Inscribes 61 New Sites on World Heritage List |url=https://whc.unesco.org/en/news/184/ |website=UNESCO World Heritage Centre |access-date=2022-05-10 |language=en |first=UNESCO World Heritage |last=Centre}}</ref>.
Additionally, the {{ill|Kunigami-utaki|ja|国仲御嶽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunigami-utaki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 六山|1=Mūyama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11392326}} on Taketomi Island, also founded by Sai Tō, enshrines the deity of Sonohyan-utaki.

It is said to have been established by {{ill|Sai Tō|ja|西塘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sai Tō|1=Sai Tō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q29413439}}, who was brought to Shuri by {{ill|Ōzato Aji|ja|大里親方|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōzato Aji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} after the {{ill|Oyake Akahachi Rebellion|ja|オヤケアカハチの乱|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyake Akahachi Rebellion|1=Oyake Akahachi rebellion|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11292491}} in 1500, during which the royal army was sent to the [[:en:Yaeyama Islands|Yaeyama Islands]].

In [[:en:1972|1972]], the gate was re-designated as an [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]. In [[:en:2000|2000]], along with the [[:en:Shuri Castle|Shuri Castle ruins]], it was included in the [[:en:Gusuku Sites and Related Properties of the Kingdom of Ryukyu|Gusuku Sites and Related Properties of the Kingdom of Ryukyu]], a [[:en:UNESCO|UNESCO]] [[World Heritage Site (UNESCO)|World Heritage Site]]<ref>{{Cite web |title=World Heritage Committee Inscribes 61 New Sites on World Heritage List |url=https://whc.unesco.org/en/news/184/ |website=UNESCO World Heritage Centre |access-date=2022-05-10 |language=en |first=UNESCO World Heritage |last=Centre}}</ref>.

'''Sonohyan-utaki''' (園比屋武御嶽) is an [[:en:Ontake|utaki]], or sacred site, from the 16th century during the [[:en:Ryukyu Kingdom|Ryukyu Kingdom]]'s [[:en:Shō Shin|King Shō Shin]] era. It is located in [[:en:Shuri, Okinawa|Shuri, Naha]], [[:en:Naha|Naha, Okinawa]], Okinawa Prefecture, and is designated as an Okinawa Prefecture Historic Site.
== Overview == The forest behind the stone gate is called '''Sonohyan-utaki'''. This utaki was a sacred place where the king would always pray before departing on journeys. Additionally, the [[:en:Kikoe-ōgimi|Kikoe-ōgimi]] performed her first prayer here upon assuming her position, making it a national {{ill|sacred site|ja|聖地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sacred site|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=sacred place|WD=Q4588528}}. The shrine enshrines the deity "Tanoue no Sonohiyabu" from Iheya Island, which was closely linked to the ruling {{ill|Shō family|ja|尚氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shō family|1=Shō family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q118441183}}. Originally, it was a much larger forest, but parts of it have since been used for a school, leaving only a portion intact.
== Sonohyan-utaki Ishimon == Located between {{ill|Kankaimon|ja|歓会門|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kankaimon|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and [[:en:Shureimon|Shureimon]] at [[:en:Shuri Castle|Shuri Castle]], the '''Sonohyan-utaki Ishimon''' (園比屋武御嶽石門) serves as the {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} of the utaki. This stone gate was built in [[:en:1519|1519]] during the reign of {{ill|King Shō Shin|ja|尚真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/King Shō Shin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 尚真王|1=Shō Shin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q281765}}, the third monarch of the {{ill|Second Shō Dynasty|ja|第二尚氏王統|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Second Shō Dynasty|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 第二尚氏|1=Second Shō Dynasty|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11602290}}. It is said to have been established by {{ill|Sai Tō|ja|西塘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sai Tō|1=Sai Tō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q29413439}}, who was brought to Shuri by {{ill|Ōzato Aji|ja|大里親方|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōzato Aji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} after the {{ill|Oyake Akahachi Rebellion|ja|オヤケアカハチの乱|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyake Akahachi Rebellion|1=Oyake Akahachi rebellion|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11292491}} in 1500, during which the royal army was sent to the [[:en:Yaeyama Islands|Yaeyama Islands]].

However, in [[:en:1957|1957]], it was reconstructed, and later, parts of the original stonework were incorporated into restoration efforts. Close inspection of the gate reveals areas with differing degrees of wear due to these reconstruction efforts. In [[:en:1972|1972]], the gate was re-designated as an [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]. In [[:en:2000|2000]], along with the [[:en:Shuri Castle|Shuri Castle ruins]], it was included in the [[:en:Gusuku Sites and Related Properties of the Kingdom of Ryukyu|Gusuku Sites and Related Properties of the Kingdom of Ryukyu]], a [[:en:UNESCO|UNESCO]] [[World Heritage Site (UNESCO)|World Heritage Site]]<ref>{{Cite web |title=World Heritage Committee Inscribes 61 New Sites on World Heritage List |url=https://whc.unesco.org/en/news/184/ |website=UNESCO World Heritage Centre |access-date=2022-05-10 |language=en |first=UNESCO World Heritage |last=Centre}}</ref>.

Additionally, the {{ill|Kunigami-utaki|ja|国仲御嶽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunigami-utaki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 六山|1=Mūyama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11392326}} on Taketomi Island, also founded by Sai Tō, enshrines the deity of Sonohyan-utaki.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Sonohyan-utaki]][[cs:Sonohjan Utaki Išimon]][[de:Sonohyan-Utaki]][[en:Sonohyan-utaki]][[fr:Sonohyan-utaki]][[ja:園比屋武御嶽]][[tr:Sonohyan-utaki]][[vi:Sonohyan-utaki]][[zh:園比屋武御嶽]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Sonohyan-utaki]][[cs:Sonohjan Utaki Išimon]][[de:Sonohyan-Utaki]][[en:Sonohyan-utaki]][[fr:Sonohyan-utaki]][[ja:園比屋武御嶽]][[tr:Sonohyan-utaki]][[vi:Sonohyan-utaki]][[zh:園比屋武御嶽]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Sonohyan-utaki]][[cs:Sonohjan Utaki Išimon]][[de:Sonohyan-Utaki]][[en:Sonohyan-utaki]][[fr:Sonohyan-utaki]][[ja:園比屋武御嶽]][[tr:Sonohyan-utaki]][[vi:Sonohyan-utaki]][[zh:園比屋武御嶽]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q1053962]] Wikidata name: Sonohyan-utaki [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:31, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox religious building |name=Sonohyan-utaki
|image=File:Naha Shuri Castle04bs3200.jpg |caption=Sonohyan-utaki Ishimon }} [[File:Sonohyan-utaki.jpeg|right|thumb|250px|The forest (utaki) behind the stone gate]]
'''Sonohyan-utaki''' (園比屋武御嶽) is an [[:en:Ontake|utaki]], or sacred site, from the 16th century during the [[:en:Ryukyu Kingdom|Ryukyu Kingdom]]'s [[:en:Shō Shin|King Shō Shin]] era. It is located in [[:en:Shuri, Okinawa|Shuri, Naha]], [[:en:Naha|Naha, Okinawa]], Okinawa Prefecture, and is designated as an Okinawa Prefecture Historic Site.
== Overview == The forest behind the stone gate is called '''Sonohyan-utaki'''. This utaki was a sacred place where the king would always pray before departing on journeys. Additionally, the [[:en:Kikoe-ōgimi|Kikoe-ōgimi]] performed her first prayer here upon assuming her position, making it a national {{ill|sacred site|ja|聖地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sacred site|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=sacred place|WD=Q4588528}}. The shrine enshrines the deity "Tanoue no Sonohiyabu" from Iheya Island, which was closely linked to the ruling {{ill|Shō family|ja|尚氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shō family|1=Shō family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q118441183}}. Originally, it was a much larger forest, but parts of it have since been used for a school, leaving only a portion intact.
== Sonohyan-utaki Ishimon == Located between {{ill|Kankaimon|ja|歓会門|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kankaimon|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and [[:en:Shureimon|Shureimon]] at [[:en:Shuri Castle|Shuri Castle]], the '''Sonohyan-utaki Ishimon''' (園比屋武御嶽石門) serves as the {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} of the utaki. This stone gate was built in [[:en:1519|1519]] during the reign of {{ill|King Shō Shin|ja|尚真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/King Shō Shin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 尚真王|1=Shō Shin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q281765}}, the third monarch of the {{ill|Second Shō Dynasty|ja|第二尚氏王統|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Second Shō Dynasty|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 第二尚氏|1=Second Shō Dynasty|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11602290}}. It is said to have been established by {{ill|Sai Tō|ja|西塘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sai Tō|1=Sai Tō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q29413439}}, who was brought to Shuri by {{ill|Ōzato Aji|ja|大里親方|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōzato Aji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} after the {{ill|Oyake Akahachi Rebellion|ja|オヤケアカハチの乱|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyake Akahachi Rebellion|1=Oyake Akahachi rebellion|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11292491}} in 1500, during which the royal army was sent to the [[:en:Yaeyama Islands|Yaeyama Islands]].
Originally designated as a [[:en:National Treasure of Japan|National Treasure]] on [[January 23, 1933]], the stone gate, along with the royal castle, was severely damaged during the [[:en:Battle of Okinawa|Battle of Okinawa]], leading to the loss of its designation. However, in [[:en:1957|1957]], it was reconstructed, and later, parts of the original stonework were incorporated into restoration efforts. Close inspection of the gate reveals areas with differing degrees of wear due to these reconstruction efforts. In [[:en:1972|1972]], the gate was re-designated as an [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]. In [[:en:2000|2000]], along with the [[:en:Shuri Castle|Shuri Castle ruins]], it was included in the [[:en:Gusuku Sites and Related Properties of the Kingdom of Ryukyu|Gusuku Sites and Related Properties of the Kingdom of Ryukyu]], a [[:en:UNESCO|UNESCO]] [[World Heritage Site (UNESCO)|World Heritage Site]]<ref>{{Cite web |title=World Heritage Committee Inscribes 61 New Sites on World Heritage List |url=https://whc.unesco.org/en/news/184/ |website=UNESCO World Heritage Centre |access-date=2022-05-10 |language=en |first=UNESCO World Heritage |last=Centre}}</ref>.
Additionally, the {{ill|Kunigami-utaki|ja|国仲御嶽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunigami-utaki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 六山|1=Mūyama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11392326}} on Taketomi Island, also founded by Sai Tō, enshrines the deity of Sonohyan-utaki.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Sonohyan-utaki]][[cs:Sonohjan Utaki Išimon]][[de:Sonohyan-Utaki]][[en:Sonohyan-utaki]][[fr:Sonohyan-utaki]][[ja:園比屋武御嶽]][[tr:Sonohyan-utaki]][[vi:Sonohyan-utaki]][[zh:園比屋武御嶽]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Sonohyan-utaki]][[cs:Sonohjan Utaki Išimon]][[de:Sonohyan-Utaki]][[en:Sonohyan-utaki]][[fr:Sonohyan-utaki]][[ja:園比屋武御嶽]][[tr:Sonohyan-utaki]][[vi:Sonohyan-utaki]][[zh:園比屋武御嶽]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Sonohyan-utaki]][[cs:Sonohjan Utaki Išimon]][[de:Sonohyan-Utaki]][[en:Sonohyan-utaki]][[fr:Sonohyan-utaki]][[ja:園比屋武御嶽]][[tr:Sonohyan-utaki]][[vi:Sonohyan-utaki]][[zh:園比屋武御嶽]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q1053962]] Wikidata name: Sonohyan-utaki [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:31, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox religious building |name=Sonohyan-utaki
|image=File:Naha Shuri Castle04bs3200.jpg |caption=Sonohyan-utaki Ishimon }} [[File:Sonohyan-utaki.jpeg|right|thumb|250px|The forest (utaki) behind the stone gate]]
'''Sonohyan-utaki''' (園比屋武御嶽) is an [[:en:Ontake|utaki]], or sacred site, from the 16th century during the [[:en:Ryukyu Kingdom|Ryukyu Kingdom]]'s [[:en:Shō Shin|King Shō Shin]] era. It is located in [[:en:Shuri, Okinawa|Shuri, Naha]], [[:en:Naha|Naha, Okinawa]], Okinawa Prefecture, and is designated as an Okinawa Prefecture Historic Site.
== Overview == The forest behind the stone gate is called '''Sonohyan-utaki'''. This utaki was a sacred place where the king would always pray before departing on journeys. Additionally, the [[:en:Kikoe-ōgimi|Kikoe-ōgimi]] performed her first prayer here upon assuming her position, making it a national {{ill|sacred site|ja|聖地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sacred site|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=sacred place|WD=Q4588528}}. The shrine enshrines the deity "Tanoue no Sonohiyabu" from Iheya Island, which was closely linked to the ruling {{ill|Shō family|ja|尚氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shō family|1=Shō family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q118441183}}. Originally, it was a much larger forest, but parts of it have since been used for a school, leaving only a portion intact.
== Sonohyan-utaki Ishimon == Located between {{ill|Kankaimon|ja|歓会門|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kankaimon|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and [[:en:Shureimon|Shureimon]] at [[:en:Shuri Castle|Shuri Castle]], the '''Sonohyan-utaki Ishimon''' (園比屋武御嶽石門) serves as the {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} of the utaki. This stone gate was built in [[:en:1519|1519]] during the reign of {{ill|King Shō Shin|ja|尚真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/King Shō Shin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 尚真王|1=Shō Shin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q281765}}, the third monarch of the {{ill|Second Shō Dynasty|ja|第二尚氏王統|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Second Shō Dynasty|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 第二尚氏|1=Second Shō Dynasty|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11602290}}. It is said to have been established by {{ill|Sai Tō|ja|西塘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sai Tō|1=Sai Tō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q29413439}}, who was brought to Shuri by {{ill|Ōzato Aji|ja|大里親方|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōzato Aji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} after the {{ill|Oyake Akahachi Rebellion|ja|オヤケアカハチの乱|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyake Akahachi Rebellion|1=Oyake Akahachi rebellion|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11292491}} in 1500, during which the royal army was sent to the [[:en:Yaeyama Islands|Yaeyama Islands]].
Originally designated as a [[:en:National Treasure of Japan|National Treasure]] on [[January 23, 1933]], the stone gate, along with the royal castle, was severely damaged during the [[:en:Battle of Okinawa|Battle of Okinawa]], leading to the loss of its designation. However, in [[:en:1957|1957]], it was reconstructed, and later, parts of the original stonework were incorporated into restoration efforts. Close inspection of the gate reveals areas with differing degrees of wear due to these reconstruction efforts. In [[:en:1972|1972]], the gate was re-designated as an [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]. In [[:en:2000|2000]], along with the [[:en:Shuri Castle|Shuri Castle ruins]], it was included in the [[:en:Gusuku Sites and Related Properties of the Kingdom of Ryukyu|Gusuku Sites and Related Properties of the Kingdom of Ryukyu]], a [[:en:UNESCO|UNESCO]] [[World Heritage Site (UNESCO)|World Heritage Site]]<ref>{{Cite web |title=World Heritage Committee Inscribes 61 New Sites on World Heritage List |url=https://whc.unesco.org/en/news/184/ |website=UNESCO World Heritage Centre |access-date=2022-05-10 |language=en |first=UNESCO World Heritage |last=Centre}}</ref>.
Additionally, the {{ill|Kunigami-utaki|ja|国仲御嶽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunigami-utaki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 六山|1=Mūyama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11392326}} on Taketomi Island, also founded by Sai Tō, enshrines the deity of Sonohyan-utaki.
== References == {{Reflist}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Sonohyan-utaki]][[cs:Sonohjan Utaki Išimon]][[de:Sonohyan-Utaki]][[en:Sonohyan-utaki]][[fr:Sonohyan-utaki]][[ja:園比屋武御嶽]][[tr:Sonohyan-utaki]][[vi:Sonohyan-utaki]][[zh:園比屋武御嶽]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Sonohyan-utaki]][[cs:Sonohjan Utaki Išimon]][[de:Sonohyan-Utaki]][[en:Sonohyan-utaki]][[fr:Sonohyan-utaki]][[ja:園比屋武御嶽]][[tr:Sonohyan-utaki]][[vi:Sonohyan-utaki]][[zh:園比屋武御嶽]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Sonohyan-utaki]][[cs:Sonohjan Utaki Išimon]][[de:Sonohyan-Utaki]][[en:Sonohyan-utaki]][[fr:Sonohyan-utaki]][[ja:園比屋武御嶽]][[tr:Sonohyan-utaki]][[vi:Sonohyan-utaki]][[zh:園比屋武御嶽]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q1053962]] Wikidata name: Sonohyan-utaki [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:31, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox religious building |name=Sonohyan-utaki
|image=File:Naha Shuri Castle04bs3200.jpg |caption=Sonohyan-utaki Ishimon }} [[File:Sonohyan-utaki.jpeg|right|thumb|250px|The forest (utaki) behind the stone gate]]
'''Sonohyan-utaki''' (園比屋武御嶽) is an {{ill|Ontake|en|Ontake|WD=Q11071176|lt=utaki}}, or sacred site, from the 16th century during the {{ill|Ryukyu Kingdom|en|Ryukyu Kingdom|WD=Q28025|lt=Ryukyu Kingdom}}'s {{ill|Shō Shin|en|Shō Shin|WD=Q281765|lt=King Shō Shin}} era. It is located in {{ill|Shuri, Okinawa|en|Shuri, Okinawa|WD=Q608415|lt=Shuri, Naha}}, {{ill|Naha|en|Naha|WD=Q181966|lt=Naha, Okinawa}}, Okinawa Prefecture, and is designated as an Okinawa Prefecture Historic Site.
== Overview == The forest behind the stone gate is called '''Sonohyan-utaki'''. This utaki was a sacred place where the king would always pray before departing on journeys. Additionally, the {{ill|Kikoe-ōgimi|en|Kikoe-ōgimi|WD=Q11611193|lt=Kikoe-ōgimi}} performed her first prayer here upon assuming her position, making it a national {{ill|sacred site|ja|聖地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sacred site|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=sacred place|WD=Q4588528}}. The shrine enshrines the deity "Tanoue no Sonohiyabu" from Iheya Island, which was closely linked to the ruling {{ill|Shō family|ja|尚氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shō family|1=Shō family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q118441183}}. Originally, it was a much larger forest, but parts of it have since been used for a school, leaving only a portion intact.
== Sonohyan-utaki Ishimon == Located between {{ill|Kankaimon|ja|歓会門|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kankaimon|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Shureimon|en|Shureimon|WD=Q713275|lt=Shureimon}} at {{ill|Shuri Castle|en|Shuri Castle|WD=Q907052|lt=Shuri Castle}}, the '''Sonohyan-utaki Ishimon''' (園比屋武御嶽石門) serves as the {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} of the utaki. This stone gate was built in {{ill|1519|en|1519|WD=Q6278|lt=1519}} during the reign of {{ill|King Shō Shin|ja|尚真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/King Shō Shin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 尚真王|1=Shō Shin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q281765}}, the third monarch of the {{ill|Second Shō Dynasty|ja|第二尚氏王統|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Second Shō Dynasty|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 第二尚氏|1=Second Shō Dynasty|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11602290}}. It is said to have been established by {{ill|Sai Tō|ja|西塘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sai Tō|1=Sai Tō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q29413439}}, who was brought to Shuri by {{ill|Ōzato Aji|ja|大里親方|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōzato Aji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} after the {{ill|Oyake Akahachi Rebellion|ja|オヤケアカハチの乱|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyake Akahachi Rebellion|1=Oyake Akahachi rebellion|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11292491}} in 1500, during which the royal army was sent to the {{ill|Yaeyama Islands|en|Yaeyama Islands|WD=Q696496|lt=Yaeyama Islands}}.
Originally designated as a [[:en:National Treasure of Japan|National Treasure]] on [[January 23, 1933]], the stone gate, along with the royal castle, was severely damaged during the {{ill|Battle of Okinawa|en|Battle of Okinawa|WD=Q192660|lt=Battle of Okinawa}}, leading to the loss of its designation. However, in {{ill|1957|en|1957|WD=Q5311|lt=1957}}, it was reconstructed, and later, parts of the original stonework were incorporated into restoration efforts. Close inspection of the gate reveals areas with differing degrees of wear due to these reconstruction efforts. In {{ill|1972|en|1972|WD=Q2476|lt=1972}}, the gate was re-designated as an {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property}}. In {{ill|2000|en|2000|WD=Q1985|lt=2000}}, along with the {{ill|Shuri Castle|en|Shuri Castle|WD=Q907052|lt=Shuri Castle ruins}}, it was included in the {{ill|Gusuku Sites and Related Properties of the Kingdom of Ryukyu|en|Gusuku Sites and Related Properties of the Kingdom of Ryukyu|WD=Q1333330|lt=Gusuku Sites and Related Properties of the Kingdom of Ryukyu}}, a {{ill|UNESCO|en|UNESCO|WD=Q7809|lt=UNESCO}} [[World Heritage Site (UNESCO)|World Heritage Site]]<ref>{{Cite web |title=World Heritage Committee Inscribes 61 New Sites on World Heritage List |url=https://whc.unesco.org/en/news/184/ |website=UNESCO World Heritage Centre |access-date=2022-05-10 |language=en |first=UNESCO World Heritage |last=Centre}}</ref>.
Additionally, the {{ill|Kunigami-utaki|ja|国仲御嶽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunigami-utaki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 六山|1=Mūyama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11392326}} on Taketomi Island, also founded by Sai Tō, enshrines the deity of Sonohyan-utaki.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == {{Commonscat|Sonohyan-utaki}} * {{ill|Ontake|en|Ontake|WD=Q11071176|lt=Utaki}} * {{ill|Gusuku Sites and Related Properties of the Kingdom of Ryukyu|en|Gusuku Sites and Related Properties of the Kingdom of Ryukyu|WD=Q1333330|lt=Gusuku Sites and Related Properties of the Kingdom of Ryukyu}} * {{OSM|n|656537494}}
{{Gusuku Sites and Related Properties of the Kingdom of Ryukyu}} {{Coord|26|13|05.1|N|127|43|02.5|E|display=title|region:JP-47_type:landmark|name=Sonohyan-utaki}}

{{infobox religious building |name=Sonohyan-utaki
|image=File:Naha Shuri Castle04bs3200.jpg |caption=Sonohyan-utaki Ishimon }} [[File:Sonohyan-utaki.jpeg|right|thumb|250px|The forest (utaki) behind the stone gate]]
'''Sonohyan-utaki''' (園比屋武御嶽) is an {{ill|Ontake|en|Ontake|WD=Q11071176|lt=utaki|1=Ontake}}, or sacred site, from the 16th century during the {{ill|Ryukyu Kingdom|en|Ryukyu Kingdom|WD=Q28025|lt=Ryukyu Kingdom|1=Ryukyu Kingdom}}'s {{ill|Shō Shin|en|Shō Shin|WD=Q281765|lt=King Shō Shin|1=Shō Shin}} era. It is located in {{ill|Shuri, Okinawa|en|Shuri, Okinawa|WD=Q608415|lt=Shuri, Naha|1=Shuri, Okinawa}}, {{ill|Naha|en|Naha|WD=Q181966|lt=Naha, Okinawa|1=Naha}}, Okinawa Prefecture, and is designated as an Okinawa Prefecture Historic Site.
== Overview == The forest behind the stone gate is called '''Sonohyan-utaki'''. This utaki was a sacred place where the king would always pray before departing on journeys. Additionally, the {{ill|Kikoe-ōgimi|en|Kikoe-ōgimi|WD=Q11611193|lt=Kikoe-ōgimi|1=Kikoe-ōgimi}} performed her first prayer here upon assuming her position, making it a national {{ill|sacred site|ja|聖地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sacred site|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Sacred space|WD=Q4588528}}. The shrine enshrines the deity "Tanoue no Sonohiyabu" from Iheya Island, which was closely linked to the ruling {{ill|Shō family|ja|尚氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shō family|1=Q118441183|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q118441183}}. Originally, it was a much larger forest, but parts of it have since been used for a school, leaving only a portion intact.
== Sonohyan-utaki Ishimon == Located between {{ill|Kankaimon|ja|歓会門|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kankaimon|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Shureimon|en|Shureimon|WD=Q713275|lt=Shureimon|1=Shureimon}} at {{ill|Shuri Castle|en|Shuri Castle|WD=Q907052|lt=Shuri Castle|1=Shuri Castle}}, the '''Sonohyan-utaki Ishimon''' (園比屋武御嶽石門) serves as the {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} of the utaki. This stone gate was built in {{ill|1519|en|1519|WD=Q6278|lt=1519|1=1519}} during the reign of {{ill|King Shō Shin|ja|尚真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/King Shō Shin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 尚真王|1=Shō Shin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q281765}}, the third monarch of the {{ill|Second Shō Dynasty|ja|第二尚氏王統|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Second Shō Dynasty|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 第二尚氏|1=Second Shō dynasty|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11602290}}. It is said to have been established by {{ill|Sai Tō|ja|西塘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sai Tō|1=Q29413439|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q29413439}}, who was brought to Shuri by {{ill|Ōzato Aji|ja|大里親方|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōzato Aji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} after the {{ill|Oyake Akahachi Rebellion|ja|オヤケアカハチの乱|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyake Akahachi Rebellion|1=Oyake Akahachi rebellion|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11292491}} in 1500, during which the royal army was sent to the {{ill|Yaeyama Islands|en|Yaeyama Islands|WD=Q696496|lt=Yaeyama Islands|1=Yaeyama Islands}}.
Originally designated as a [[:en:National Treasure of Japan|National Treasure]] on [[January 23, 1933]], the stone gate, along with the royal castle, was severely damaged during the {{ill|Battle of Okinawa|en|Battle of Okinawa|WD=Q192660|lt=Battle of Okinawa|1=Battle of Okinawa}}, leading to the loss of its designation. However, in {{ill|1957|en|1957|WD=Q5311|lt=1957|1=1957}}, it was reconstructed, and later, parts of the original stonework were incorporated into restoration efforts. Close inspection of the gate reveals areas with differing degrees of wear due to these reconstruction efforts. In {{ill|1972|en|1972|WD=Q2476|lt=1972|1=1972}}, the gate was re-designated as an {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}. In {{ill|2000|en|2000|WD=Q1985|lt=2000|1=2000}}, along with the {{ill|Shuri Castle|en|Shuri Castle|WD=Q907052|lt=Shuri Castle ruins|1=Shuri Castle}}, it was included in the {{ill|Gusuku Sites and Related Properties of the Kingdom of Ryukyu|en|Gusuku Sites and Related Properties of the Kingdom of Ryukyu|WD=Q1333330|lt=Gusuku Sites and Related Properties of the Kingdom of Ryukyu|1=Gusuku Sites and Related Properties of the Kingdom of Ryukyu}}, a {{ill|UNESCO|en|UNESCO|WD=Q7809|lt=UNESCO|1=UNESCO}} [[World Heritage Site (UNESCO)|World Heritage Site]]<ref>{{Cite web |title=World Heritage Committee Inscribes 61 New Sites on World Heritage List |url=https://whc.unesco.org/en/news/184/ |website=UNESCO World Heritage Centre |access-date=2022-05-10 |language=en |first=UNESCO World Heritage |last=Centre}}</ref>.
Additionally, the {{ill|Kunigami-utaki|ja|国仲御嶽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunigami-utaki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 六山|1=Mūyama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11392326}} on Taketomi Island, also founded by Sai Tō, enshrines the deity of Sonohyan-utaki.
== References == {{Reflist}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Sonohyan-utaki]][[cs:Sonohjan Utaki Išimon]][[de:Sonohyan-Utaki]][[en:Sonohyan-utaki]][[fr:Sonohyan-utaki]][[ja:園比屋武御嶽]][[tr:Sonohyan-utaki]][[vi:Sonohyan-utaki]][[zh:園比屋武御嶽]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Sonohyan-utaki]][[cs:Sonohjan Utaki Išimon]][[de:Sonohyan-Utaki]][[en:Sonohyan-utaki]][[fr:Sonohyan-utaki]][[ja:園比屋武御嶽]][[tr:Sonohyan-utaki]][[vi:Sonohyan-utaki]][[zh:園比屋武御嶽]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:Sonohyan-utaki]][[cs:Sonohjan Utaki Išimon]][[de:Sonohyan-Utaki]][[en:Sonohyan-utaki]][[fr:Sonohyan-utaki]][[ja:園比屋武御嶽]][[tr:Sonohyan-utaki]][[vi:Sonohyan-utaki]][[zh:園比屋武御嶽]]
{{infobox religious building |name=Sonohyan-utaki
|image=File:Naha Shuri Castle04bs3200.jpg |caption=Sonohyan-utaki Ishimon }} [[File:Sonohyan-utaki.jpeg|right|thumb|250px|The forest (utaki) behind the stone gate]]
'''Sonohyan-utaki''' (園比屋武御嶽) is an {{ill|Ontake|en|Ontake|WD=Q11071176|lt=utaki|1=Ontake}}, or sacred site, from the 16th century during the {{ill|Ryukyu Kingdom|en|Ryukyu Kingdom|WD=Q28025|lt=Ryukyu Kingdom|1=Ryukyu Kingdom}}'s {{ill|Shō Shin|en|Shō Shin|WD=Q281765|lt=King Shō Shin|1=Shō Shin}} era. It is located in {{ill|Shuri, Okinawa|en|Shuri, Okinawa|WD=Q608415|lt=Shuri, Naha|1=Shuri, Okinawa}}, {{ill|Naha|en|Naha|WD=Q181966|lt=Naha, Okinawa|1=Naha}}, Okinawa Prefecture, and is designated as an Okinawa Prefecture Historic Site.
== Overview == The forest behind the stone gate is called '''Sonohyan-utaki'''. This utaki was a sacred place where the king would always pray before departing on journeys. Additionally, the {{ill|Kikoe-ōgimi|en|Kikoe-ōgimi|WD=Q11611193|lt=Kikoe-ōgimi|1=Kikoe-ōgimi}} performed her first prayer here upon assuming her position, making it a national {{ill|sacred site|ja|聖地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sacred site|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Sacred space|WD=Q4588528}}. The shrine enshrines the deity "Tanoue no Sonohiyabu" from Iheya Island, which was closely linked to the ruling {{ill|Shō family|ja|尚氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shō family|1=Q118441183|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q118441183}}. Originally, it was a much larger forest, but parts of it have since been used for a school, leaving only a portion intact.
== Sonohyan-utaki Ishimon == Located between {{ill|Kankaimon|ja|歓会門|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kankaimon|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Shureimon|en|Shureimon|WD=Q713275|lt=Shureimon|1=Shureimon}} at {{ill|Shuri Castle|en|Shuri Castle|WD=Q907052|lt=Shuri Castle|1=Shuri Castle}}, the '''Sonohyan-utaki Ishimon''' (園比屋武御嶽石門) serves as the {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} of the utaki. This stone gate was built in {{ill|1519|en|1519|WD=Q6278|lt=1519|1=1519}} during the reign of {{ill|King Shō Shin|ja|尚真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/King Shō Shin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 尚真王|1=Shō Shin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q281765}}, the third monarch of the {{ill|Second Shō Dynasty|ja|第二尚氏王統|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Second Shō Dynasty|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 第二尚氏|1=Second Shō dynasty|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11602290}}. It is said to have been established by {{ill|Sai Tō|ja|西塘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sai Tō|1=Q29413439|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q29413439}}, who was brought to Shuri by {{ill|Ōzato Aji|ja|大里親方|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōzato Aji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} after the {{ill|Oyake Akahachi Rebellion|ja|オヤケアカハチの乱|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyake Akahachi Rebellion|1=Oyake Akahachi rebellion|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11292491}} in 1500, during which the royal army was sent to the {{ill|Yaeyama Islands|en|Yaeyama Islands|WD=Q696496|lt=Yaeyama Islands|1=Yaeyama Islands}}.
Originally designated as a [[:en:National Treasure of Japan|National Treasure]] on [[January 23, 1933]], the stone gate, along with the royal castle, was severely damaged during the {{ill|Battle of Okinawa|en|Battle of Okinawa|WD=Q192660|lt=Battle of Okinawa|1=Battle of Okinawa}}, leading to the loss of its designation. However, in {{ill|1957|en|1957|WD=Q5311|lt=1957|1=1957}}, it was reconstructed, and later, parts of the original stonework were incorporated into restoration efforts. Close inspection of the gate reveals areas with differing degrees of wear due to these reconstruction efforts. In {{ill|1972|en|1972|WD=Q2476|lt=1972|1=1972}}, the gate was re-designated as an {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}. In {{ill|2000|en|2000|WD=Q1985|lt=2000|1=2000}}, along with the {{ill|Shuri Castle|en|Shuri Castle|WD=Q907052|lt=Shuri Castle ruins|1=Shuri Castle}}, it was included in the {{ill|Gusuku Sites and Related Properties of the Kingdom of Ryukyu|en|Gusuku Sites and Related Properties of the Kingdom of Ryukyu|WD=Q1333330|lt=Gusuku Sites and Related Properties of the Kingdom of Ryukyu|1=Gusuku Sites and Related Properties of the Kingdom of Ryukyu}}, a {{ill|UNESCO|en|UNESCO|WD=Q7809|lt=UNESCO|1=UNESCO}} [[World Heritage Site (UNESCO)|World Heritage Site]]<ref>{{Cite web |title=World Heritage Committee Inscribes 61 New Sites on World Heritage List |url=https://whc.unesco.org/en/news/184/ |website=UNESCO World Heritage Centre |access-date=2022-05-10 |language=en |first=UNESCO World Heritage |last=Centre}}</ref>.
Additionally, the {{ill|Kunigami-utaki|ja|国仲御嶽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunigami-utaki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 六山|1=Mūyama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11392326}} on Taketomi Island, also founded by Sai Tō, enshrines the deity of Sonohyan-utaki.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == {{Commonscat|Sonohyan-utaki}} * {{ill|Ontake|en|Ontake|WD=Q11071176|lt=Utaki|1=Ontake}} * {{ill|Gusuku Sites and Related Properties of the Kingdom of Ryukyu|en|Gusuku Sites and Related Properties of the Kingdom of Ryukyu|WD=Q1333330|lt=Gusuku Sites and Related Properties of the Kingdom of Ryukyu|1=Gusuku Sites and Related Properties of the Kingdom of Ryukyu}} * {{OSM|n|656537494}}
